rows.title: Pest

text_1.eng: A pest is any insect, disease, or animal that harms your crops and livestock.

rows.text_1: A pest is any insect, disease, or animal that harms your crops and livestock.

rows.title: Pest Management

text_1.eng: Pest management uses natural and chemical tools to monitor and control pests before they cause damage.

text_2.eng: The goal is to protect your crops and livestock while keeping your farm healthy and profitable.

rows.text_1: Pest management uses natural and chemical tools to monitor and control pests before they cause damage.
rows.text_2: The goal is to protect your crops and livestock while keeping your farm healthy and profitable.

section_1_text.eng:
* Aphids
 * Whiteflies
 * Bollworms.
* Rodents and birds.

section_1_extra_text.eng:
* Bore holes in plants
 * Harm roots
 * Steal nutrients from your plants.

rows.section_1_text:
* Aphids
 * Whiteflies
 * Bollworms.
* Rodents and birds.
rows.section_1_extra_text:
* Bore holes in plants
 * Harm roots
 * Steal nutrients from your plants.

section_1_text.eng:
* Rotate crops and plant at the right time.
 * Grow pest-repellent crops (e.g., onions).
 * Use healthy seeds, fertilizers, and resistant crop varieties.
 * Remove weeds and diseased plants.
 * Harvest on time before pests attack.

rows.section_1_text:
* Rotate crops and plant at the right time.
 * Grow pest-repellent crops (e.g., onions).
 * Use healthy seeds, fertilizers, and resistant crop varieties.
 * Remove weeds and diseased plants.
 * Harvest on time before pests attack.

rows.title: Biological Control

text_1.eng: This maintains a healthy balance on your farm without using toxic chemicals.

section_1_text.eng: * Use nature to fight pests by encouraging “good insects”

section_1_extra_text.eng:
* Ladybirds feeds on aphids
 * Wasps feeds on insects
 * Cat eats rats

section_2_extra_text.eng:
*  Ash
 * Soap
 * Ash brew
 * Hot pepper.

section_3_text.eng: * Plant flowers to attract beneficial insects.

rows.title: Ash Brew

text_1.eng: It reinforces crop bio-protection especially for leaves.

media_transcript.eng:
* Bring water to a boil.
 * Grate the soap and add to water, don't stir until soap is completely melted.
 * Add the ash and stir for 15 minutes(sprinkle cold water on the mix if it starts to boil over).
 * Remove from fire
 * Let it cool down and it is ready for use

rows.text_1: It reinforces crop bio-protection especially for leaves.
rows.media_transcript:
* Bring water to a boil.
 * Grate the soap and add to water, don't stir until soap is completely melted.
 * Add the ash and stir for 15 minutes(sprinkle cold water on the mix if it starts to boil over).
 * Remove from fire
 * Let it cool down and it is ready for use

rows.title: Ash Brew Ingredients

section_1_text.eng: * 10 kg of ash (well sifted) * 1 kg of bar soap (avoid soaps with cemicals and detergents) * 40 litres of water

section_1_extra_text.eng:
* Stick to stir
 * One vat or metal barrel
 * Firewood

rows.section_1_text: * 10 kg of ash (well sifted) * 1 kg of bar soap (avoid soaps with cemicals and detergents) * 40 litres of water
rows.section_1_extra_text:
* Stick to stir
 * One vat or metal barrel
 * Firewood

rows.title: Ash Brew Storage

section_1_text.eng:
* Take out liquid and store in a drum.
 * Use remaining solids for washing dishes.

section_2_text.eng:
* Dilute 3-5 litres in 100 litres of water (3-5%) and use as foliar spray
 * Apply when the weather is cool (i.e. evening or morning)
 * Add 2 litres of cooking oil immediately after brewing 
 * Oil makes it more efficient in controlling insects with waxy body or scales
 * This practice increases photosynthesis rates

rows.section_1_text:
* Take out liquid and store in a drum.
 * Use remaining solids for washing dishes.
rows.section_2_text:
* Dilute 3-5 litres in 100 litres of water (3-5%) and use as foliar spray

 * Oil makes it more efficient in controlling insects with waxy body or scales
 * This practice increases photosynthesis rates

section_1_text.eng:
* Use scarecrows to keep birds away.
 * Set traps and use nets, screens, or fences.
 * Pick pests off by hand.

rows.section_1_text:
* Use scarecrows to keep birds away.
 * Set traps and use nets, screens, or fences.
 * Pick pests off by hand.

section_1_text.eng:
* Dry maize and cereals well properly to make them hard for weevils not to attack.
 * Store in hermetic bags or silos to prevent pests.

rows.title: Botanical Methods

text_1.eng: Involves using plant extracts as natural insecticides to control pests.

section_1_text.eng: These plants have chemicals that make crops taste bad to pests, so they stay away.
